description_long: This is a two-story wooden house with a gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The exterior features horizontal wood siding in two tones: a lighter beige section and a warmer, reddish-brown section, creating visual contrast. The front facade includes a small covered porch with a sloped roof, supported by simple posts, and a main entry door. Windows are rectangular and symmetrically arranged on the front and rear, with a mix of single and double units, and a small attic window under the gable. The house sits on a raised foundation above a neatly mowed green lawn. The overall design is simple and functional, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on practicality.
description: A two-story wooden house with a gable roof, contrasting wood siding, a small covered porch, and symmetrically arranged windows, set on a green lawn.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch, three windows on the upper floor, and two on the lower floor, with contrasting wood siding.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long wall with three windows and the covered porch extending from the right side.
description_view_2: Rear view with a similar gable end, three upper windows, and two lower windows, but no porch.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a single window on the lower floor and the full length of the gable roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple covered porch with a sloped roof, supported by posts, and a single main door.
